response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile full-stack engineer, but the role demands 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, which is absent here. C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and an academic thesis, far short of the requirement. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB/generic SQL), no fixed income or capital-markets C++ work, and the one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was Node/TypeScript, not C++. The profile points toward web/backend in TypeScript and DLang rather than the C++ capital-markets systems work described.
strengths: Broad, deep software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in low-level/performance-oriented work
Fintech-adjacent exposure at RIDE Capital with strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) and dated, well below the required 5–10 years
No SQL Server/Oracle, no fixed income or investment-banking C++ domain experience
Core stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang, not Windows C++ for capital-markets systems
